Team: # This tab is the LIVE source for every team's Financial Statement (tabs 7+). Each row is ONE line item.
# Columns: Team | Section | Item | Amount | Cashflow | Rate | Note.

# Section vocabulary (exactly): Income | Skill | Skill Up | Expense | Asset | Opportunity - Small | Opportunity - Big | Liability. The statement FILTERs by Team + Section, showing only the rows that belong to that team.

# TWO GROUPS behave alike: ASSET-TYPE = Asset + Opportunity - Small + Opportunity - Big (value under Assets, Cashflow under Income, COUNTS toward Passive Income/escaping). INCOME-TYPE = Income + Skill + Skill Up (Amount under Income; active income only, does NOT count toward Passive Income).
# Amount = the figure shown IN that section: Income/payday, Expense/payday, Asset value, Liability balance.

#   Asset / Opportunity - Small / Opportunity - Big row -> passive income/payday it pays (0 for net-worth assets like a laptop). It surfaces in the INCOME section and counts toward escaping the rat race.
#   Liability row -> monthly payment/payday. It surfaces in the EXPENSES section. THIS IS A FORMULA = Amount x Rate (see below).
#   Income / Expense rows -> leave Cashflow blank.
# Rate = the debt's monthly rate, used ONLY on Liability rows: 0.1 for 10% consumer debt (gear, credit card, BNPL, phone, forced personal loans), 0.025 for the 2.5% student loan.

# AUTO DEBT PAYMENT: each Liability row's Cashflow cell is the formula =Amount*Rate (e.g. row 12: =D12*F12). So you only ever edit the BALANCE (Amount) - the monthly payment and the Expenses line recalculate themselves.

#   If after import a Cashflow cell shows the text '=D12*F12' instead of a number, your import didn't convert formulas: just retype the '=' or re-import with 'Convert text to numbers/formulas' enabled.
#   Keep balances on the $500 (10%) / $2000 (2.5%) grid so the auto payment lands on clean $50 steps.

# SKILLS are INCOME-TYPE rows (Amount = the income bump; leave Cashflow blank). Use Section 'Skill' for a BOARD Skill-Up space (Online Course +150, Professional Certification +300, Coding Bootcamp +450, Marketing Course +600) and Section 'Skill Up' for a LIFE-HAPPENS skill card (+100). Both grow the payday but do NOT count toward Passive Income / escaping - only Asset / Opportunity rows and stock dividends do.
# STOCKS are the exception - they are auto-derived from the Holdings + Market Prices tabs, so do NOT add stock rows here.

#   Buy a board asset -> add an 'Asset' row; buy an Opportunity deal -> add an 'Opportunity - Small' or 'Opportunity - Big' row (Amount = value/cost, Cashflow = its per-payday income) + log the cash row.
#   Gain a board Skill-Up -> add a 'Skill' row; gain a Life-Happens skill card -> add a 'Skill Up' row (Amount = the income bump, Cashflow blank) + log the cash row.
#   Take on a new liability -> add a Liability row (Amount = balance, Rate = 0.1 or 0.05, Cashflow = =Amount*Rate) + log any cash received.
#   A 'balance grows' card (loan/card interest) -> just raise that Liability's Amount; the payment updates itself. NO cash row.
#   Repay debt -> just lower that Liability's Amount (balance) by the amount repaid; the payment updates itself + log the cash row.
#   Sell an asset -> delete its row (or set Amount to 0) + log the cash row.
# Do not insert/rename columns A-E - the statement formulas key off those positions (Item=C, Amount=D, Cashflow=E). Rate=F and Note=G are free for you to use.
# Rule of Zeroes: keep all figures multiples of $50 / $100.
